title: Package and document agent-session-search for clean local setup
description:
Context/background:
The core search_sessions MCP path is implemented. The remaining V1 risk is packaging and setup quality: a future agent should be able to clone this repository, install dependencies, build it, wire it into an MCP client, and run a smoke search without relying on Ben's local memory or untracked dot-agent files.

Current packaging findings from review:
- package.json advertises bin entries at ./dist/cli.js and ./dist/server.js, but the TypeScript build currently emits dist/src/cli.js and dist/src/server.js. An installed or linked package would expose broken binaries.
- npm pack --dry-run currently includes local agent/tooling clutter such as .agents/, .beads/.br_history/, skills-lock.json, source tests, and built tests because there is no package allowlist or .npmignore.
- README.md is stale: it still says the backend is follow-up work, while the FFF backend and smoke path now exist.
- FFF is an external binary dependency. fff-mcp is available locally, but it is not published as an npm package. Official FFF install guidance is a curl installer, so this repo needs explicit setup/preflight handling instead of pretending npm install is sufficient.

Scope:
- Make the built package/install surface real: package bin entries match emitted files, installed commands execute, and package contents are intentionally allowlisted.
- Add a pragmatic FFF setup/preflight path that verifies fff-mcp and, where appropriate, points to the official installer.
- Document installation, build/test/smoke commands, config file shape, source filtering, missing-root warnings, MCP registration, and manual CLI usage.
- Verify the documented flow from the perspective of a clean clone/local install.
- Keep the public MCP surface centered on the single search_sessions tool.

Out of scope:
- Publishing to npm unless explicitly requested.
- Replacing FFF with a custom indexer or rebuilding CASS.
- A full website or broad user guide.
- Project-aware ranking beyond a roadmap note.

Reasoning:
A local MCP server is only useful if another agent can set it up correctly. Packaging correctness is now a product requirement, not just documentation polish.
acceptance_criteria:
- package.json bin entries and build output agree, and both CLI and MCP entrypoints can be executed after npm run build.
- npm pack --dry-run includes only intentional package files and excludes local agent/tooling artifacts, bead history, source tests, and built tests.
- Setup/preflight guidance handles the fff-mcp external binary dependency explicitly and does not claim npm install alone installs FFF.
- README or equivalent docs explain install, test, build, run, MCP registration, config, source filtering, missing-root warnings, and manual smoke searches.
- Config example includes codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes roots plus include patterns.
- Docs state that CASS is not required and must not be run for this tool.
- Verification is performed with the documented commands before closing the epic.
notes: Implementation should be split across child beads so each part can be reviewed independently. Close this epic only after all children are closed and final verification passes.
close_reason: All packaging child beads are closed; final verification passed with npm run check, npm test, npm run build, npm run smoke, npm run check:fff, npm pack --dry-run --json, and installed package bin coverage.

title: Build search_sessions fanout coordinator and response shaping
description:
Context/background:
The V1 user-facing behavior is one MCP tool, search_sessions, that searches broadly across configured source roots and returns expanded patterns, searched source status, warnings, and raw-ish hits. This bead connects root resolution and the one-root backend into the first useful multi-source search.

Scope:
- Implement the shared search_sessions library function behind the MCP tool.
- Use the root resolver to choose roots and report searchedSources.
- Use an identity/original-query pattern expansion initially if the query rewriter bead is not complete. The response must still populate expandedPatterns.
- Fan out searches across selected roots and patterns.
- Enforce maxResultsPerSource, context, and debug behavior.
- Combine partial results and warnings without failing the whole request when at least one source succeeds.
- Return a clear all-sources-failed error/warning shape, including an rg fallback suggestion if appropriate.

Out of scope:
- Sophisticated query rewriting.
- Project-aware ordering.
- Session-level aggregation or ranking.

Reasoning:
This is the first practical MCP search path. It should be able to search everything quickly even if query expansion is still simple.
acceptance_criteria:
- search_sessions can search multiple selected roots.
- The common request { query } works with defaults.
- sources, maxPatterns, maxResultsPerSource, context, and debug are honored where implemented.
- Missing roots and per-source backend failures are represented as warnings while successful sources still return hits.
- expandedPatterns is present even when it only contains the original query.
- results stay raw-ish and do not aggregate sessions or invent custom ranking.
- The MCP server returns the same response shape as the shared library.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Use fake resolver/backend tests for multi-root fanout, partial failure, all-source failure, caps, warnings, and output shape.
- Add a behavior test for the MCP tool handler calling this coordinator.
close_reason: Implemented search_sessions fanout coordinator with TDD; verified npm run check, npm test, and npm run build

title: Scaffold MCP server, shared library, and CLI entrypoints
description:
Context/background:
This repo needs the fastest useful V1 of agent-session-search: a local MCP server exposing search_sessions for coding-agent session history. The repo currently has a PRD but little implementation, so this bead establishes the minimal runnable project shape without overbuilding. The implementation should support a shared library that both the MCP server and optional CLI can call.

Scope:
- Choose a minimal runtime/tooling stack appropriate for an MCP server. The PRD examples use TypeScript, so TypeScript/Node is the expected default unless implementation discovery finds a better existing convention.
- Add the project/package scaffold needed to run tests and start the MCP server locally.
- Create a shared library boundary for search_sessions behavior, even if the implementation initially returns a stub.
- Create an MCP server entrypoint that registers the search_sessions tool with the agreed request/response shape.
- Create a minimal CLI entrypoint or script hook that can call the same library path for local smoke testing.

Out of scope:
- Real FFF search.
- Full query rewriting.
- Project-aware ranking.
- Beads workflow documentation setup.

Reasoning:
The first useful vertical path needs a process that can be launched as MCP and a library seam that tests can target. Keep the scaffold boring and small so later beads can fill in behavior without reshaping the public API.
acceptance_criteria:
- The repo has a runnable MCP server entrypoint.
- The MCP server registers a search_sessions tool with input fields: query, sources, maxPatterns, maxResultsPerSource, context, debug.
- The shared library exposes one public search function or equivalent deep interface for the tool to call.
- A CLI/script path can invoke the same library for manual smoke testing.
- Basic test command and build/typecheck command exist, even if initial tests are small.
- Documentation or package scripts explain how to start the MCP server locally.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Add at least one behavior-level test that proves the MCP/tool handler calls the shared search library and returns JSON-structured output.
- Prefer testing the library/tool boundary, not private implementation details.
close_reason: Completed scaffold using FastMCP. Verified npm run check, npm test, npm run build, and npm run dev:cli -- "auth token timeout" --json.

title: Wrap one-root FFF MCP backend
description:
Context/background:
FFF is the search engine. fff-mcp accepts one optional root path, so agent-session-search must create one backend per root and normalize each backend's results. This bead builds the smallest useful one-root backend before fanout.

Scope:
- Implement a backend abstraction that searches one canonical root through one child fff-mcp process.
- Send literal search patterns to the child backend using the MCP stdio protocol or the smallest reliable local integration discovered during implementation.
- Convert backend results into raw normalized hits with source, root, path, line, content, pattern, and optional context.
- Preserve backend warnings/errors as structured warnings.
- Enforce per-root timeout/cap behavior so a stuck child does not hang the full search.
- Clean up child process resources.

Out of scope:
- Multi-root fanout.
- Query rewriting beyond receiving literal patterns.
- Session parsing or aggregation.

Reasoning:
This is the critical integration risk. Prove one root works before coordinating all roots.
acceptance_criteria:
- A backend can search one root for one or more literal patterns.
- Returned hits include canonical absolute path, root, source, line when available, content, matched pattern, and context when requested/available.
- Backend failure returns a structured warning/error to the caller instead of crashing the process.
- Timeout or process failure cannot hang the search indefinitely.
- The backend can be tested with a fake child/MCP client without requiring a live FFF index.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Unit-test result normalization and warning conversion with a fake backend/client.
- Add one integration-smoke test path if a reliable fff-mcp invocation is available in the local environment; skip or isolate it if not available.
close_reason: Implemented one-root FFF backend with MCP adapter, timeout/error warnings, result caps, cleanup, fake-client tests, and live fff-mcp smoke coverage. Verified with npm test, npm run check, and npm run build.

title: Ship prototype-backed search pipeline improvements
description:
## Outcome
Ship the prototype-backed search pipeline improvements as a reviewable sequence of implementation and investigation Beads.

## Background
- Source plan: `docs/plans/search-pipeline-prototype-synthesis.md`.
- Fanout finding: `docs/prototypes/findings/fanout-prototype-findings.md`.
- Ranking finding: `docs/prototypes/findings/recency-ranking-prototype-findings.md`.
- Pre-chain guard: `bd-5se` locks unscoped evidence grouping edge cases before broader `test/search.test.ts` churn.
- Final decisions:
  - FFF remains the search backend.
  - MCP remains centered on the single `search_sessions` tool.
  - Prototype code is reference material, not mainline implementation.
  - Result shapes stay close to current FFF/search response contracts.
  - Candidate/evidence grouping stays by `source + path`.
  - Missing source coverage is investigated separately from ranking policy.

## Closure contract
Do not close this parent until every direct child below is closed or explicitly closed as unnecessary with evidence.

Direct children:
- `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff` - harden custom backend cleanup failure handling before fanout.
- `bd-3tv.1` - parallel source fanout with deterministic slot merge.
- `bd-3tv.2` - recency plus hit-density candidate ranking and current-session demotion.
- `bd-3tv.3` - bounded project-aware candidate ranking preference.
- `bd-9na` - debug-only ranking explanations.
- `bd-3tv.4` - archived/source-coverage gap decision.

## Children / intended order
0. `bd-5se`: external guard bead that should land first to avoid `test/search.test.ts` collisions and pin evidence grouping before search-pipeline edits.
1. `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff`: establishes cleanup failure semantics while the search path is still sequential and reviewable.
2. `bd-3tv.1`: fanout changes shared `src/search.ts` coordination and preserves the cleanup behavior from the prerequisite.
3. `bd-3tv.2`: core candidate ranking depends on stable fanout order and cap semantics.
4. `bd-3tv.3`: project-aware preference builds on the ranking model and must stay conservative about metadata.
5. `bd-9na`: debug explanations follow project-aware ranking so they explain the final ranking factors.
6. `bd-3tv.4`: independent investigation; it may run in parallel and must create follow-up Beads before product source-coverage changes.

## Scope
- Search coordination improvements backed by the fanout prototype.
- Candidate ordering improvements backed by the recency-ranking prototype.
- Bounded project-aware preference when safe project signals exist.
- Debug-only observability for candidate ranking factors.
- Focused public-API tests in `test/search.test.ts`.
- Small docs updates only when they describe accepted product behavior or the source-coverage decision.

## Non-goals
- No new MCP tools.
- No custom index, embeddings, SQLite search store, markdown exports, or session aggregation.
- No Pool source split.
- No arbitrary transcript-content parsing for ranking metadata.
- No throwaway prototype harnesses or raw review transcripts in product code/history.
- No CASS/archive integration without a separate implementation Bead and design decision.

## Parent acceptance criteria
- All direct child Beads close with verification evidence or unnecessary-work rationale.
- Public `search_sessions` response compatibility is preserved except intentional candidate ordering changes.
- Warning order remains root-resolution, source warnings in configured order, then global warnings.
- Canonical absolute paths plus `source` and `root` metadata remain attached to results.
- Candidate ranking stays deterministic, score-free in normal responses, and isolated from evidence modes.
- The one-tool MCP boundary and FFF-backed design remain intact.
- `bd-3tv.4` has either produced follow-up Beads for source coverage or closed with an evidence-backed no-change/defer decision.

## Validation
```bash
br dep cycles --json
bv --robot-insights
bv --robot-plan
npm run check
npm test
```
Docs/investigation-only children may close with br/bv validation, docs evidence, and a note explaining skipped code tests.

## Parallelization notes
- Treat `src/search.ts` and `test/search.test.ts` as single-owner surfaces by default.
- `bd-5se -> bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff -> bd-3tv.1 -> bd-3tv.2 -> bd-3tv.3 -> bd-9na` is the stacked code/test lane.
- `bd-3tv.4` may run in parallel because it should not edit ranking/fanout behavior.
- Within this parent lane, the only current `ready-for-agent` child should be `bd-3tv.4`; `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff` becomes ready after `bd-5se` closes.
- Graph-level ready work may also include independent beads such as cross-agent signal research when their docs/surfaces do not collide.

## Closure evidence
Close this parent with child statuses, final validation results, deferred follow-up Beads, and confirmation no prototype-only files/scripts were promoted.

title: Parallelize source fanout with deterministic merge
description:
## Outcome
`searchSessions` searches resolved `ok` roots in concurrent source slots while preserving ordered MCP/CLI results, warnings, and caps.

## Source
- Parent: `bd-3tv`; depends on `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff`.
- Preserve: FFF backend, one `search_sessions` tool, `source + path` grouping, result shapes.
- Decision: private fanout only. No public concurrency knob, env var, or request field.

## Success criteria
- Missing, disabled, unselected, and unreadable roots behave as today and do not create backends.
- Resolved `ok` roots run in independent slots; defaults overlap.
- Any private limiter preserves default overlap and source-order merge.
- Slot input preserves patterns, request cap, context, optional `paths`, and `include`.
- Each slot returns index, status/warning, warnings, hits, cap state, failure state, and cleanup state.
- Each slot catches create/search/cleanup work so siblings always settle.
- Filtering, truncation, query attribution, and per-source caps happen before ordered merge.
- Merge hits by configured source index and keep backend order within each source.
- Preserve `searchedSources`, attempted/failed counts, `all_sources_failed`, `broad_evidence_capped`, focused caps, and result shapes.
- Preserve cleanup-warning behavior from `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff`.
- Warning order remains root-resolution warnings, per-source warnings in configured order, then global warnings.
- Default FFF pool teardown stays in `CoordinatedSessionSearch.close()`.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do refactor `CoordinatedSessionSearch.searchSessions` around slots plus deterministic merge.
- Do add public-API tests in `test/search.test.ts`; close with MCP/CLI smoke coverage.
- Do not change ranking, rewriting, grouping, debug payload shape, normal fields, FFF, MCP boundary, public concurrency config, debug timings, or prototype files.
- Do not redesign cleanup warnings; preserve the prerequisite contract.

## Failure behavior
- `createBackend` or `search` failure remains source-local and must not cancel siblings.
- Backend failure warning with no retained hits marks that source failed and contributes to all-failed accounting.
- Backend warning with retained hits preserves current partial-success behavior and keeps hits.
- Cleanup failures follow `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff`.
- `all_sources_failed` only appears when every attempted source failed and no retained raw hits remain.
- No attempted source, broad cap, and focused evidence preserve current behavior.

## Anchors
- `src/search.ts`: `searchSessions`, cap/filter/shape helpers.
- `src/client-pool.ts`: `createFffBackendPool`.
- Tests: `test/search.test.ts`, `test/mcp-smoke.test.ts`, `test/cli.test.ts`.
- Finding: `docs/prototypes/findings/fanout-prototype-findings.md`.

## Validation
```bash
npm run check
npm test
npm run smoke
npm run dev:cli -- "auth token timeout" --json
```
Test intent:
- Prove overlap without brittle wall-clock assertions.
- Prove backend-input parity plus warning order, failure isolation, caps, cleanup, and pooled-close ownership.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff`.
- Blocks: `bd-3tv.2` and parent `bd-3tv`.
- Single-owner risk: `src/search.ts` and `test/search.test.ts`.
- `bd-3tv.4` may run in parallel if it stays to docs/source-coverage investigation.

## Closure evidence
Close with commands/results, commit, slot/merge summary, concurrency decision, cleanup preservation, smoke evidence, and follow-up Beads.

title: Rank default candidates by recency and hit density
description:
## Outcome
Default candidate results use deterministic recency/density ranking, with identifiable live-session echoes demoted below historical candidates.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: `bd-3tv`; depends on `bd-3tv.1`.
- Source: search-pipeline synthesis plan and recency-ranking prototype findings.
- Reference branch: `codex/prototype-recency-ranking`.
- Preserve: FFF backend, one `search_sessions` tool, `source + path` grouping, score-free normal responses.

## Success criteria
- Candidate grouping remains by `source + path`.
- Ranking happens after fanout/filtering and candidate grouping.
- Ranking applies only to candidates; evidence groups and path-restricted evidence hits keep existing order.
- Candidate mtimes come from filesystem metadata on canonical candidate paths, not transcript parsing.
- Failed `stat`, missing files, stale paths, or synthetic paths are non-fatal and get no recency boost.
- Formula:
  - `recencyPoints`: `<=2h` 4, `<=24h` 3, `<=7d` 2, `<=30d` 1, older/missing 0.
  - `densityPoints`: `min(log2(hitCount + 1), 4)`.
  - `score`: `recencyPoints * 2 + densityPoints`.
  - Sort: non-current first, score desc, hit count desc, mtime desc, original order.
- Current Codex sessions are demoted when `CODEX_THREAD_ID` matches candidate `sessionId`.
- Tests may use a private injection/test hook; do not add a public MCP field for this slice.
- Broader agent-specific current-session IDs are deferred to `bd-cross-agent-current-session-demotion-mzt`.
- Normal candidate payloads expose no scores, mtimes, or ranking factors.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do implement ranking in the production candidate path after fanout lands.
- Do use prototype code only as reference and add focused public-API ranking tests.
- Do not add embeddings, a new index, CASS/archive coverage, transcript summarization, transcript parsing, project-aware ranking, debug explanations, or a new MCP current-session field.
- Do not change evidence mode ordering to make candidate ranking easier.

## Failure behavior
- Missing/stale result paths do not fail search.
- No current-session id means no demotion.
- If no mtimes can be collected, preserve existing candidate order via original-order tie-breaks.
- Ties stay deterministic after score, hit count, mtime, and original order.
- Mtime errors do not add user-visible warnings unless debug-only diagnostics are added later.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- User-visible: default MCP/CLI candidate ordering.
- `src/search.ts`: `shapeResults`, `toCandidates`, `toEvidenceGroups`, `sessionIdFromPath`.
- `src/types.ts`: `SearchCandidate`, `SearchSessionsInput`, `SearchSessionsOutput`.
- `test/search.test.ts`: public `createSessionSearch` fixtures.
- Prototype names: `collectCandidateMtimes`, `candidateRank`, `recencyBucketPoints`, `orderCandidates`.

## Validation
```bash
npm run check
npm test
```
Behavior-test intent:
- Dense recent beats fresh one-hit noise.
- Hot dense working session beats slightly denser same-day history.
- Current session loses despite many echo hits.
- Missing `mtime` falls back.
- Evidence modes and normal candidate payloads remain unchanged.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: `bd-3tv.1`.
- Blocks: `bd-3tv.3` and parent `bd-3tv`.
- Single-owner risk: ranking internals in `src/search.ts` and tests in `test/search.test.ts`.

## Closure evidence
Close with commands/results, commit hash, scoring constants, current-session id source, evidence-mode confirmation, and remaining follow-ups.

title: Add project-aware candidate ranking preference
description:
## Outcome
Candidate ranking gives a bounded preference to sessions with safe evidence that they belong to the caller's current project.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: `bd-3tv`; depends on `bd-3tv.2`.
- Source: search-pipeline synthesis plan and recency-ranking prototype findings.
- Preserve: normal candidate payloads stay score-free; missing project signals are a no-op.

## Success criteria
- Accept project signals only from structured string fields in `operationalContext`, such as `cwd`, `projectRoot`, `workspace`, or `repo`.
- Canonicalize path-like fields when possible; failures are non-fatal.
- Derive conservative project tokens from canonical project paths/repo names only.
- Ignore generic/broad tokens such as `home`, `users`, `data`, `projects`, `src`, `tmp`, and one-character tokens.
- Candidate project matches come from path/root metadata or conservative source-path tokens, not transcript content, snippets, or previews.
- Source formats with no safe project metadata, such as date-only Codex session paths, may receive no project boost.
- Project boost composes with `bd-3tv.2` and is at most one recency bucket: no more than 2 score points.
- Strong cross-project evidence can still win when recency/density is materially better.
- Absent, malformed, unrelated, or non-string context preserves deterministic core ranking.
- Normal candidates expose no scores, project factors, or ranking internals.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do add project-aware preference after core ranking exists.
- Do test exact project matches, conservative token matches if implemented, unrelated fresh sessions, malformed context, generic tokens, and unboostable sources.
- Do not add CASS/archive coverage, debug fields, full transcript parsers, normal response-shape changes, or transcript-body inference.
- Do not make project preference strong enough to bury materially better historical matches.

## Failure behavior
- Malformed `operationalContext` is ignored safely.
- Missing project signals fall back to core ranking.
- Path canonicalization failures fall back to safe token/path comparison or no boost.
- Candidate metadata extraction failure leaves that candidate unboosted.
- False-positive prevention beats recall; ambiguous or broad repo tokens should not boost.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Input: `SearchSessionsInput.operationalContext` in `src/types.ts`; `searchSessionsInputSchema` in `src/tool.ts`.
- CLI: `--cwd`, `--branch`, `--reason` in `src/cli.ts`; only `cwd` is a default project signal unless another field is accepted.
- Ranking path: `src/search.ts` helpers from `bd-3tv.2`.
- Root behavior: `src/roots.ts`, especially canonical roots and include patterns.
- Tests/design: `test/search.test.ts` and `DESIGN.md`.

## Validation
```bash
npm run check
npm test
```
Behavior-test intent:
- Safe current-project match beats a similar unrelated fresh candidate.
- Materially stronger cross-project candidate can still win.
- Malformed/unrelated context preserves order.
- Sources without safe metadata are not guessed from transcript text.
- Generic tokens do not boost.
- Normal mode remains score-free.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: `bd-3tv.2`.
- Blocks: `bd-9na` and parent `bd-3tv`.
- Single-owner risk: ranking helpers, context parsing, and shared tests.

## Closure evidence
Close with commands/results, commit hash, accepted signal fields, boost size, examples of boostable/unboostable sources, and follow-up Beads if needed.

title: Evaluate archived session source coverage gap
description:
## Outcome
Document whether sessions found by CASS but missed by configured live FFF roots need product, config, or upstream follow-up.

## Context
- Parent: `bd-3tv`.
- Sources: search-pipeline synthesis plan and recency-ranking prototype findings.
- Local memory: CASS 0.6.5 can be healthy/searchable even when `cass doctor --check` reports `degraded-archive-risk` solely because old Codex sessions moved from `~/.codex/sessions` to `~/.codex/archived_sessions`.
- Preserve: FFF remains the search engine unless `DESIGN.md` changes.

## Success criteria
- Check known missing-answer paths against live roots, Codex archives, CASS, and effective config, including `AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH_CONFIG`.
- Run or document why unavailable: `npm run dev:cli -- sources --json`, `cass health`, `cass doctor --check`, and targeted `cass search ... --json` probes.
- For the known Codex archive warning, verify `cass health`, search behavior, and raw mirror/archive evidence before calling CASS broken.
- Decide whether misses are absent from live roots, archived elsewhere, config-recoverable, realpath/include related, or not reproducible.
- Add `docs/prototypes/findings/source-coverage-gap-decision.md` or an equivalently named findings doc.
- Recommend one path with evidence: config guidance, built-in root adjustment, CASS/archive design, upstream CASS archive-root recognition, defer, or no change.
- Create follow-up Beads before changing product behavior.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do capture durable commands, probes, config/root observations, and CASS interpretation.
- Do treat verified Codex raw-mirror sole-copy warnings as conservative CASS coverage noise.
- Do not add CASS integration, archive indexing, embeddings, SQLite stores, markdown exports, durable aggregation, ranking changes, Pool source splits, or personal config mutation.

## Failure behavior
- Missing archives, unavailable `cass`, or unavailable local roots produce documented evidence, not partial implementation.
- Healthy `cass health` plus verified search/raw mirror evidence overrides the known Codex-only doctor warning.
- Broader CASS failures or inconclusive evidence become a narrower `needs-info`/investigation follow-up.
- If no product change is right, close with evidence and rationale.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Missing Codex session ids:
  - `019e3f4c-81e2-75a0-a125-8ea2ea42dd9f`
  - `019e421a-f5fb-73b3-9d32-41f99f621639`
- Codex archive root: `/home/ben/.codex/archived_sessions`.
- Product/config: `src/roots.ts`, `DESIGN.md`, `~/.config/agent-session-search/config.json`.
- Durable output: `docs/prototypes/findings/source-coverage-gap-decision.md`.

## Validation
```bash
br dep cycles --json
bv --robot-insights
bv --robot-plan
npm run check:beads
```
Docs-only closure may skip `npm test` with a reason; code/config changes require `npm run check` and `npm test`.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Can run in parallel with ranking/fanout only if it edits docs or creates follow-up Beads.
- Must not edit `src/search.ts` ranking behavior.
- May run in parallel with `bd-cross-agent-current-session-demotion-mzt` if each owns a distinct findings doc.
- May carry `ready-for-agent`.

## Closure evidence
Close with decision doc path, probes run, config/root observations, CASS health/search/raw-mirror interpretation, follow-up Beads, and validation.

title: Lock unscoped evidence grouping edge cases
description:
## Outcome
Unscoped evidence mode has regression coverage for edge cases where grouping could accidentally collapse or report the wrong result shape.

## Source of truth
- Preserves: `search_sessions` evidence grouping by `source + path`, not path alone.
- Preserves: empty successful evidence searches return an evidence-groups envelope, not candidates or raw hits.
- Related older bead merged here: `bd-mrd`.
- Sequencing: land before `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff` and the `bd-3tv` search-pipeline chain to reduce `test/search.test.ts` collision risk.

## Success criteria
- Add public-API coverage for two sources returning hits with the same canonical path string.
- Same-path multi-source results produce two `SearchEvidenceGroup` entries with distinct `source` and `root` values.
- Each group keeps correct `hitCount`, snippets, matched patterns/queries, and scoped `more.evidence` payload.
- Add public-API coverage for unscoped `resultsDisplayMode: "evidence"` when the backend succeeds with no hits.
- Empty unscoped evidence returns `resultsShape: "evidence_groups"`, `results: []`, and no warnings.
- Existing candidate, focused-evidence, cap, include, and warning behavior remains unchanged.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do add focused tests for the public `createSessionSearch` behavior.
- Do not test private helpers such as `toEvidenceGroups` directly.
- Do not change production behavior unless a regression test exposes a real bug.
- Do not alter ranking, fanout, evidence caps, MCP schema, or the one-tool boundary.

## Failure behavior
- If same-path groups collapse across sources, fix grouping so source metadata is preserved.
- If empty unscoped evidence returns the wrong shape or warnings, fix the result-shaping path.
- If the tests already pass, close with evidence and no production-code change.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Public API: `createSessionSearch` and `searchSessions({ resultsDisplayMode: "evidence" })`.
- `src/search.ts`: `shapeResults`, `toEvidenceGroups`, `evidenceFollowup`.
- `src/types.ts`: `SearchEvidenceGroup`, `SearchSessionsOutput.resultsShape`.
- `test/search.test.ts`: existing evidence grouping, focused evidence, cap, and include tests.

## Validation
```bash
npm run check
npm test
```
Behavior-test intent:
- Same canonical path from two sources remains two evidence groups.
- Empty successful unscoped evidence uses `evidence_groups` with no warnings.
- Existing evidence tests stay green.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Independent product behavior, but intentionally precedes the `bd-3tv` implementation chain to avoid `test/search.test.ts` edit races.
- Blocks: `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff`.
- Single-owner risk: `test/search.test.ts`; do not run in parallel with `bd-backend-cleanup-failure-5ff`, `bd-3tv.1`, or ranking/debug test edits.
- Can be implemented before fanout as long as tests assert public behavior.

## Closure evidence
Close with commands/results, commit hash, whether production code changed, and confirmation both merged edge cases are covered.
close_reason: Added public createSessionSearch regression coverage for same canonical path across multiple sources and empty successful unscoped evidence. Production code unchanged; existing source+path grouping and evidence_groups shaping already satisfied behavior. Validation: npm run check; npm test; npm test -- test/search.test.ts.

title: Expand README for install, config, MCP registration, and smoke usage
description:
Context/background:
README.md currently documents only the basic dev commands and contains stale language saying the backend is follow-up work. The docs need to make a clean clone usable by another agent without consulting beads or local memory.

Scope:
- Document prerequisites, npm install/build/test/smoke commands, and the FFF preflight/setup path.
- Document ~/.config/agent-session-search/config.json with roots for codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes, including include patterns.
- Document source filtering and missing/unreadable root warnings.
- Document MCP registration using the built server entrypoint and the package bin command where applicable.
- Document manual CLI smoke searches and the important response fields.
- State clearly that CASS is not required and must not be run for this tool.

Out of scope:
- Full website or long-form user guide.
- Beads workflow documentation.
acceptance_criteria:
- README no longer contains stale backend-scaffold language.
- README explains install, check, test, build, smoke, FFF preflight/setup, MCP run, and CLI usage.
- README includes a config JSON example with codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes roots plus include patterns.
- README explains sources: all/default behavior and source-specific filtering.
- README explains missing-root warnings and partial-success behavior.
- README explicitly says CASS is not part of this tool and should not be run.
close_reason: Expanded README setup docs and added README acceptance coverage

title: Verify clean clone/local install packaging flow
description:
Context/background:
The packaging epic should close only after the documented setup flow has been tested from the perspective of someone cloning the repo or installing the local package. This bead is the final proof pass.

Scope:
- Run the documented verification commands after the package and docs beads land.
- Include npm run check, npm test, npm run build, npm run smoke, FFF preflight, npm pack --dry-run, and a local package install/link binary check where practical.
- Confirm the package contents do not include local dot-agent artifacts or built/source tests.
- Update docs or packaging if verification finds stale instructions.

Out of scope:
- Publishing to a registry.
- Live searches across every real agent session corpus.
acceptance_criteria:
- Documented commands have been followed locally.
- npm run check, npm test, npm run build, and npm run smoke pass.
- FFF preflight/setup command behaves as documented.
- npm pack --dry-run package contents are clean and intentional.
- Local package binary checks prove agent-session-search and agent-session-search-mcp resolve to working entrypoints.
- Any verification caveats are captured before closing the epic.
close_reason: Verified clean local packaging flow: check, test, build, smoke, FFF preflight, dry-run tarball contents, and installed package bin coverage all pass; documented tarball/local install verification commands.

title: Expose debug-only ranking explanations
description:
## Outcome
Debug-enabled candidate searches expose compact ranking explanations without changing normal candidate responses.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: `bd-3tv`; depends on `bd-3tv.3`.
- Source: search-pipeline synthesis plan and recency-ranking prototype findings.
- Explain: recency, density, current-session demotion, and project preference.
- Preserve: current `resultsDisplayMode: "debug"` compatibility unless this bead explicitly updates docs and tests.

## Success criteria
- Attach ranking explanations only when the shaped result is candidates.
- Candidate searches with `debug: true` expose `debug.ranking.candidates` mirroring returned candidate order.
- Normal non-debug candidate responses remain unchanged and score-free.
- CLI/MCP `resultsDisplayMode: "debug"` behavior is not silently repurposed; compatibility changes are explicit in tests/docs.
- Each debug entry includes: rank, source, path, optional sessionId, hitCount, originalIndex, isCurrentSession, optional mtimeMs, recencyBucket, recencyPoints, densityPoints, projectMatch, projectPoints, and score.
- `recencyBucket`: `lt_2h`, `lt_24h`, `lt_7d`, `lt_30d`, or `older_or_missing`.
- `projectMatch`: `none`, `path`, `repo_token`, or `other_safe_metadata`.
- Debug ranking data omits transcript content, previews, and evidence snippets.
- Output is deterministic for missing mtimes, absent/malformed project context, and current-session demotion.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do add debug-only ranking observability and focused tests.
- Do not add a second MCP tool, CLI-only ranking endpoint, source-coverage work, project-signal expansion, normal response ranking fields, or ranking policy changes.
- Do not expose transcript text, previews, snippets, or backend raw hit content inside ranking explanations.

## Failure behavior
- Missing metadata for one candidate yields missing/zero factor fields, not search failure.
- Ranking explanation failure preserves result order and normal candidate payloads.
- If a safe debug-only diagnostic location exists, explanation failure may be reported there.
- Explanation failure should not add normal user-visible warnings.
- Malformed `operationalContext` remains a ranking/debug no-op.
- Missing path mtimes remain non-fatal.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `src/search.ts`: debug payload construction and ranking helpers.
- `src/types.ts`: `SearchSessionsOutput.debug` and candidate contracts.
- `src/tool.ts`: `debug` boolean and `resultsDisplayMode` schema docs.
- `src/cli.ts` / `src/help.ts`: `--debug`, `--mode debug`, and capabilities docs.
- `test/search.test.ts`: public debug and candidate tests.
- `DESIGN.md`: one MCP tool, FFF-shaped output.

## Validation
```bash
npm run check
npm test
```
Behavior-test intent:
- Candidate search with `resultsDisplayMode: "candidates"` and `debug: true` explains returned order/factors.
- Normal candidate mode stays score-free.
- Existing `resultsDisplayMode: "debug"` tests stay green or receive explicit docs updates.
- Evidence/debug raw-hit shapes do not gain candidate-only ranking arrays by accident.
- Malformed context and missing mtimes are non-fatal.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: `bd-3tv.3`.
- Blocks: parent `bd-3tv`.
- Single-owner risk: ranking/debug helpers, shared output typing, CLI docs, and debug tests.

## Closure evidence
Close with commands/results, commit hash, final debug field names, request shapes receiving `debug.ranking`, and any debug-mode compatibility note.

title: Harden custom backend cleanup failure handling
description:
## Outcome
Custom backend `close()` failures are source-local cleanup warnings; they do not reject successful `searchSessions`, hide hits, or trigger false all-source failure.

## Context
- Parent: `bd-3tv`; prerequisite for `bd-3tv.1` fanout.
- Depends on: `bd-5se`, so evidence grouping tests land before broader `test/search.test.ts` edits.
- Current anchor: `src/search.ts` awaits `backend?.close?.()` in `finally`; a custom backend close throw can currently escape after search work.
- Preserve: pooled FFF teardown stays owned by `CoordinatedSessionSearch.close()`.

## Success criteria
- Custom backend `close()` is awaited after backend creation.
- Hits and normal shaping survive cleanup failure.
- Cleanup failure emits `source_cleanup_failed` with source, root, and error message.
- Cleanup-only failure does not mark the source failed and does not emit `all_sources_failed`.
- Search failure plus cleanup failure preserves the original search warning/status and records cleanup evidence.
- `createBackend` failure before backend creation reports only `source_search_failed`.
- Default pooled FFF clients are not closed per search.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do harden cleanup in the current sequential search path.
- Do add public-API regression tests in `test/search.test.ts`.
- Do not parallelize fanout, change ranking/caps/debug shape, alter FFF pool ownership, or change the one-tool MCP boundary.

## Failure behavior
- Cleanup after hits: return hits and add `source_cleanup_failed`.
- Cleanup after search throw: return both warnings in deterministic source-local order.
- Create failure with no backend: no cleanup warning.
- Unexpected cleanup-warning construction failure must not throw away the search result/failure outcome.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `src/search.ts`: `CoordinatedSessionSearch.searchSessions` cleanup path.
- `src/types.ts`: `SearchWarning`, `SearchedSource`.
- `test/search.test.ts`: fake backend/source failure fixtures.
- `src/client-pool.ts`: centralized FFF pool lifecycle.

## Validation
```bash
npm run check
npm test
```
Test intent: cleanup throw after hits, cleanup throw after search throw, create failure without backend, no cleanup-only `all_sources_failed`, and pooled teardown unchanged.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: `bd-5se`.
- Blocks: `bd-3tv.1` and parent `bd-3tv`.
- Single-owner risk: `src/search.ts` and `test/search.test.ts`.

## Closure evidence
Close with commands/results, commit hash, cleanup warning shape, and pooled teardown confirmation.

title: Investigate cross-agent current-session demotion signals
description:
## Outcome
Decide how `search_sessions` should identify and demote the caller's current live session outside Codex without adding unsafe public API surface or transcript parsing.

## Source of truth
- Deferred from: `bd-3tv.2` core recency/density ranking.
- Prototype finding: `docs/prototypes/findings/recency-ranking-prototype-findings.md`.
- Current scoped behavior: Codex current-session demotion may use `CODEX_THREAD_ID` when it matches a candidate `sessionId`.
- Preserve: single `search_sessions` MCP tool, FFF backend, and score-free normal candidate payloads.

## Success criteria
- Inventory plausible current-session identifiers for supported agents: Codex, Claude, Cursor, Pi, Hermes, and Pool.
- Distinguish reliable environment/client signals from unsafe guesses based on transcript text or path coincidence.
- Capture the decision in `docs/prototypes/findings/current-session-demotion-signals.md` or an equivalently named findings doc.
- Recommend one of:
  - implement additional safe signals,
  - keep Codex-only demotion for now,
  - add an explicit design note for why this is not reliable yet.
- If implementation is recommended, create or update implementation Beads with exact signal fields, failure behavior, tests, and non-goals.
- If no implementation is recommended, close with evidence and a no-change rationale.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do investigate and document the cross-agent signal contract.
- Do inspect current local/session-path conventions enough to classify each supported agent.
- Do not implement ranking changes in this investigation bead.
- Do not add a new MCP tool, transcript parser, embedding/index, custom store, or normal response field.
- Do not infer currentness from transcript body content, previews, snippets, or user query text.

## Failure behavior
- Missing or undocumented agent signals lead to documented uncertainty, not guessed demotion.
- If a signal can identify another session incorrectly, prefer no demotion.
- If evidence is inconclusive, leave this bead `needs-info` or create a narrower investigation bead naming the missing fact.
- If local roots or agent installs are absent, record that absence as evidence instead of substituting assumptions.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Ranking contract: `bd-3tv.2`.
- Candidate session id extraction: `src/search.ts` `sessionIdFromPath`.
- Input/context contracts: `src/types.ts`, `src/tool.ts`, `src/cli.ts`.
- Root conventions: `src/roots.ts`, `CONTEXT.md`, and `DESIGN.md`.
- Durable output: `docs/prototypes/findings/current-session-demotion-signals.md`.

## Validation
```bash
br dep cycles --json
bv --robot-insights
bv --robot-plan
npm run check:beads
```
Docs-only investigation can close without `npm test`; implementation follow-ups must define code validation.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Does not block `bd-3tv`; it is deferred scope outside the prototype-backed production lane.
- May run in parallel with `bd-5se` and `bd-3tv.4` if it owns only its findings doc and any follow-up Beads.
- Should not run in parallel with code implementation of `bd-3tv.2` unless the agent only documents signal research.
- Single-owner risk: any future current-session ranking helper in `src/search.ts`; this bead should not edit it.

## Closure evidence
Close with signal inventory, findings doc path, recommendation, linked follow-up Beads if any, validation commands, and rationale for unsupported agents.
close_reason: Completed docs-only investigation in docs/prototypes/findings/current-session-demotion-signals.md. Recommendation: keep current-session demotion Codex-only for now; CODEX_THREAD_ID is the only verified exact current-session signal matching indexed local candidate sessionId. Claude/Pi have transcript ids but no verified current runtime signal; Cursor resume chat ids, Pool history latest, cwd/mtime/content matches, and Claude remote session ids are not safe local demotion signals. Validation: br dep cycles --json passed with 0 cycles; bv --robot-insights and bv --robot-plan ran; npm run check:beads failed because tracked .beads/issues.jsonl has unstaged bead state changes from active claims/closeout, with no commit/stage performed.

title: Add FFF dependency preflight and setup path
description:
Context/background:
agent-session-search shells out to the external fff-mcp binary through the MCP stdio client. fff-mcp is not currently published as an npm package, so npm install cannot be the whole setup story. The repo needs an explicit way for a future agent to detect whether FFF is present and know how to install it.

Scope:
- Add a setup/preflight command or script that checks for fff-mcp on PATH and reports its version or health status when available.
- When fff-mcp is missing, print actionable install guidance using the official FFF MCP installer URL and mention reviewing the script first if desired.
- Avoid silently running remote curl installers during npm install.
- Document any relevant environment variables such as AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH_FFF_DB_DIR and AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH_CONFIG.

Out of scope:
- Vendoring FFF.
- Reimplementing FFF with rg, SQLite, embeddings, or CASS.
acceptance_criteria:
- A documented command verifies fff-mcp availability and fails with a clear message when it is missing.
- The command succeeds on a machine where fff-mcp is installed and shows enough information to debug PATH/version issues.
- Missing FFF guidance points to the official FFF MCP installer and does not imply npm install installs FFF.
- The server/CLI behavior remains unchanged except for clearer setup diagnostics where appropriate.
close_reason: Implemented FFF preflight command, docs, and tests

title: Implement deterministic query rewriter
description:
Context/background:
The PRD calls for natural-language queries to expand into a small set of FFF-friendly literal patterns. For the fastest initial MCP path, this can trail the identity/original-query fallback, but it remains part of the desired V1 completeness.

Scope:
- Implement deterministic query rewriting with no model calls.
- Emit exact phrases, file paths, branch names, PR/issue/bead IDs, commands, test names, error strings, stack trace fragments, symbols, package names, configured synonyms, and obvious snake/camel/kebab variants.
- Drop broad generic terms such as bug, code, problem, and thing.
- Respect maxPatterns and fall back to the original query when rewriting produces nothing.
- Integrate with search_sessions expandedPatterns.

Out of scope:
- Semantic/vector search.
- Model-based rewriting.
- Custom ranking.

Reasoning:
This improves recall, but the initial useful search can ship with original-query matching first. Keep this bead lower priority so it does not block the MCP scaffold and fanout path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Rewriter emits 1 to maxPatterns literal patterns for representative natural-language prompts.
- Exact quoted phrases, paths, commands, error fragments, and IDs are preserved.
- Generic filler terms are not emitted as standalone patterns.
- Synonym config is applied when present.
- Empty/failed rewrites fall back to the original query.
- search_sessions uses the rewriter when available and still reports expandedPatterns.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Unit-test representative queries from the PRD and edge cases.
- Tests should assert output patterns, caps, and fallback behavior, not private parser internals.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic query rewriting with tests

title: Add agent-planned session search probes
description:
Context/background:
agent-session-search is an agent-facing recall tool, not a human search box. Agents often receive conversational user requests plus operational context. The MCP surface should let the calling LLM agent provide planned literal probes and lightweight context instead of forcing the tool to infer everything from one yappy query.

Scope:
- Add optional planned query/probe input to search_sessions while keeping query as the original request.
- Add optional operational context metadata for audit/debug and agent guidance.
- Fan out planned probes through existing deterministic mechanical expansion and preserve original query/pattern provenance.
- Update MCP tool description/README/PRD to instruct agents to provide context/probes when useful.

Acceptance criteria:
- Existing single-query callers keep working.
- Planned probes are searched in addition to or instead of deterministic fallback as documented.
- Output/debug exposes enough provenance for an agent to see what was searched.
- Tests cover the public search_sessions behavior and MCP input parsing.

comments.author: ben
comments.text: Implemented agent-planned queries plus operationalContext through the public search_sessions API/MCP schema; updated PRD/README/tool description. Verified with npm run check, npm test, npm run check:fff, and npm run build.

title: Add empty unscoped evidence resultsShape regression test
description: Add a small public-API regression test for an unscoped resultsDisplayMode: evidence search whose backend returns no hits. Assert resultsShape is evidence_groups, results is an empty array, and warnings remain empty when the backend succeeds. This covers the empty-group case without testing private helpers such as toEvidenceGroups directly.
close_reason: Merged into bd-5se so both unscoped evidence edge cases land as one reviewable public-API regression-test packet: same-path multi-source grouping plus empty evidence_groups shape. No functionality dropped.

title: Implement config and root resolver for session sources
description:
Context/background:
search_sessions must search multiple real source roots while preserving canonical paths and handling missing roots gracefully. Supported built-in sources are codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes. Roots are configurable, but V1 needs sensible defaults for Ben's machine.

Scope:
- Define source names and root config shape for built-in and custom sources.
- Load config from ~/.config/agent-session-search/config.json when present.
- Provide default roots for codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes as described in PRD.md.
- Apply source filtering from search_sessions input, including sources: "all" and custom source names.
- Validate root existence/readability and return searchedSources entries with status ok, missing, or failed plus structured warnings.
- Preserve include patterns in config, including Cursor's */agent-transcripts/* shape.

Out of scope:
- Actually searching files.
- Ranking project-local results.
- Creating AGENTS.md or Matt-skill workflow docs.

Reasoning:
Root resolution is a high-leverage seam. It lets the MCP tool search all configured session stores while tolerating machines that only have some agents installed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Built-in defaults exist for /Users/ben/.codex/sessions, /Users/ben/.claude/projects, /Users/ben/.pi/agent/sessions, /Users/ben/.cursor/projects, and /Users/ben/.hermes/sessions.
- Configured roots can override or extend defaults.
- Missing roots produce warnings and do not fail the whole request.
- Source filtering selects only requested sources.
- Custom source names are accepted at runtime.
- Cursor include patterns are represented so downstream search can avoid irrelevant files.
- Output includes searchedSources with canonical root paths where possible.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Unit-test defaults, config override, source filtering, missing roots, unreadable roots where practical, and custom sources.
- Use temporary directories for behavior tests instead of depending on Ben's real home paths.
close_reason: Implemented config/root resolver with built-in roots, config merge, source filtering, canonical paths, and missing-root warnings. Verified npm test, npm run check, npm run build, and CLI root status smoke.

title: Add end-to-end search_sessions smoke path
description:
Context/background:
The fast V1 needs evidence that the MCP server can be launched and search_sessions can execute through the real entrypoint. This bead adds a small smoke path after the core coordinator exists.

Scope:
- Add a command or script that exercises search_sessions end to end through the public entrypoint.
- Prefer a deterministic fixture root for automated smoke tests.
- Also document a manual local command for searching real configured sources.
- Confirm the output includes query, expandedPatterns, searchedSources, warnings, and results.

Out of scope:
- Exhaustive E2E coverage of all real agent session directories.
- Performance benchmarking.
- Project-aware relevance ordering.

Reasoning:
This prevents a false V1 where unit tests pass but the MCP process or CLI path cannot actually run.
acceptance_criteria:
- A smoke command runs search_sessions through the public entrypoint.
- The smoke path uses a deterministic fixture or temporary session root in automation.
- A manual command is documented for searching real configured roots.
- Smoke output demonstrates the required response fields.
- The test/verification command is included in package scripts or docs.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Add an automated smoke test using fixtures.
- Keep live-home-directory searches manual so tests do not depend on Ben's current session corpus.
close_reason: Implemented MCP search_sessions smoke path with deterministic fixture root and documented smoke command

title: Fix package build output, bin entries, and tarball contents
description:
Context/background:
The current package is not installable as advertised. package.json maps binaries to ./dist/cli.js and ./dist/server.js, but npm run build emits dist/src/cli.js and dist/src/server.js. npm pack --dry-run also includes untracked local agent/tooling files and test/build artifacts because there is no explicit package allowlist.

Scope:
- Align TypeScript build output and package.json bin entries so agent-session-search and agent-session-search-mcp execute after npm run build and after local package installation/linking.
- Add an explicit package contents policy, preferably package.json files or .npmignore, so packed artifacts are intentional.
- Exclude local agent/tooling directories, bead history, skills-lock.json, tests, and built tests from package output.
- Keep source layout boring and avoid broad refactors.

Out of scope:
- Publishing to npm.
- Changing the public search_sessions MCP API.
acceptance_criteria:
- npm run build emits the files referenced by package.json bin entries, or package.json is updated to match the emitted files.
- Built CLI and MCP entrypoints execute directly with node after npm run build.
- Local package install/link exposes working agent-session-search and agent-session-search-mcp commands.
- npm pack --dry-run excludes .agents/, .claude/, .factory/, .goose/, .pi/, skills/, skills-lock.json, .beads/.br_history/, source tests, and dist/test.
- npm pack --dry-run output is reviewed before closing.
close_reason: Fixed build output, executable npm bin entrypoints, and package allowlist. Verified npm run check, npm test, npm run build, and npm pack --dry-run.
